reason: The snippet exists exactly at RIGHT:4 in the diff; the mutable default argument `defaults={}` is a genuine Python anti-pattern that risks silent state sharing across call sites.
comment: Mutable default argument: `defaults={}` is evaluated once at function definition time and the same dict object is reused across every call that omits the argument. If this function is ever extended to write back into `defaults`, those writes will silently persist across call sites. The idiomatic fix is `defaults=None` with `if defaults is None: defaults = {}` at the top of the body.
